dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: The live root TanStack app has a recognizable direction: routes and components import shared UI, auth, realtime, and API helpers; the helpers do not import routes or feature components.
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: The active backend under server/Sheshi.Api is mostly decomposed by recognizable responsibilities: Auth, Data, Domain, Email, Features, Realtime, and Storage.
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Root frontend modules follow predictable TanStack conventions: route files export `Route = createFileRoute(...)`, feature components use named component exports, and shared UI primitives live under `s
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: src/lib/api-client.ts centralizes HTTP failure handling: api() throws ApiError for non-2xx responses, toApiError preserves status and parsed payload when possible, and apiJson/apiForm build on that si
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: src/lib/sheshi.ts:45-151 uses API helper names that map directly to product actions: listRooms, getRoomBySlug, createRoom, listMessages, postMessage, toggleVote, softDeleteMessage, submitReport.
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: src/lib/api-client.ts:22-71 keeps HTTP behavior in three small layers: raw authenticated fetch with token refresh, JSON body/response handling, and FormData handling; each layer adds behavior rather t
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json has a cohesive main runtime stack around React 19, TanStack Start/Router/Query, Tailwind 4, SignalR, and shadcn/Radix primitives.
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: Phase 1 positive: server/Sheshi.Api/Features/Messages/MessageService.cs keeps enrichment as a single direct sequence of grouped lookups followed by DTO projection; state is local to dictionaries and a
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: [+] src/lib/api-client.ts centralizes base URL handling, bearer-token attachment, refresh-token retry, JSON/form request handling, and ApiError construction, so most frontend-to-backend handoffs enter
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: server/Sheshi.Api.Tests/ApiFactory.cs boots the ASP.NET API through WebApplicationFactory against a throwaway PostgreSQL Testcontainers database, so backend tests exercise migrations, seeding, routing
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: src/lib/sheshi.ts exposes a mostly regular async domain wrapper surface: list/get/create/post/toggle/delete/report functions all return Promises and use typed DTO-like inputs for create/post/report.
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: alb_sheshi/server/Sheshi.Api/Features/Messages/MessagesController.cs protects message writes with [Authorize] and then consistently checks current user and banned status for create/upvote paths; delet
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Application-facing files such as src/lib/sheshi.ts, src/lib/api-client.ts, src/components/MessageCard.tsx, and src/routes/tema.$messageId.tsx are mostly terse and purpose-driven, with little comment n
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: The repository contains two runnable frontend surfaces: the root TanStack/Vite app in package.json with dev/build scripts at lines 6-12, and a separate alb_sheshi/frontend Vite app with its own dev/bu
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: holistic_context.structure shows the main TanStack app is predictably layered: src/routes imports src/components 14 times, src/components/ui 17 times, src/lib 15 times, src/hooks 8 times, and src/i18n
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: src/lib/config.server.ts:19-26 deliberately exposes getServerConfig() and reads process.env inside the function, matching the file comment that Cloudflare-bound env should not be captured at module sc
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: The TanStack route files in src/routes generally keep route-level loading, local UI state, and page rendering together without creating extra indirection; src/routes/index.tsx is long enough to trigge
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: src/lib/sheshi.ts keeps read APIs honest: getRoomBySlug, getMessage, and getThread all advertise Promise<... | null> and convert only 404 ApiError responses to null while rethrowing other failures.
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: src/routes/dhoma.$slug.tsx uses a local cancelled flag before setting room state after getRoomBySlug resolves, showing the codebase has an established pattern for stale async route loads.
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: Root TypeScript is compiled with strict: true in tsconfig.json, and npm run build completed successfully, so the active frontend has a real type-checking baseline.

review_issue_summaries.summary: Two divergent app/backend trees make ownership and change targets ambiguous
The repo contains two divergent Sheshi application roots with overlapping ownership.
Top-level project contracts do not clearly describe the canonical app structure.
Parallel root and `alb_sheshi` app trees expose divergent conventions for the same domains
Background fetch failures are repeatedly converted into empty or not-found UI states
Domain helpers replace structured ApiError context with bare Error message sentinels
Composer doSubmit hides that it posts a message and clears composer state
Form/schema packages are direct runtime dependencies without source usage
A large shadcn/Radix dependency kit remains installed for unused UI wrappers
Two frontend package roots carry overlapping dependencies with different declared versions
Legacy App.tsx keeps too many local effects, handlers, and duplicated UI internals in one component body
Realtime hub lifecycle and protocol details are duplicated in route components
Message workflows are orchestrated inside the controller instead of a focused application service
Frontend-critical API, auth, realtime, route, and component behavior has no automated test harness
The duplicate alb_sheshi frontend keeps a large monolithic app path with no tests
Message listing APIs expose arrays in one app surface and cursor pages in the parallel app surface
Frontend API methods place auth tokens in different positions across similar calls
Duplicate server trees disagree on who may create rooms
Role checks mix shared constants with magic role strings
Unused generated UI wrappers add thousands of lines of template noise to production paths
Two runnable app/backend trees preserve divergent migration states and API contracts
A divergent alb_sheshi subtree duplicates the canonical frontend/backend layout
SSR error capture depends on importing a module for global listener registration
API startup always performs database migration and seed work before serving requests
Large unused chart component adds unearned UI surface
Large unused sidebar module obscures the actual navigation design
Legacy App.tsx mixes application orchestration and most views in one file
apiJson<T> can return undefined despite promising Promise<T>
Getter-style exports hide initialization side effects
Thread reload can apply a stale getThread result after navigation changes messageId
Overlapping auth loads can restore a user after tokens were replaced or cleared
API facade wrappers are marked async even when they only return another promise
Root frontend API models mark server-guaranteed response fields as optional
Moderation report DTO weakens domain enums into raw strings
